commit.message:
feat: L1 provider detection, SelectMethod enforcement, ContosoUniversity Run 19 (#425)

* feat: Post-merge validation with 100% acceptance tests

- Branch recreated from dev (not main) per team directive
- All 155 dev-docs files restored from feature branch
- AfterContosoUniversity migrated app restored
- ContosoUniversity.AcceptanceTests restored (40 tests)

Fixed issue: Students without enrollments now appear in GridView
- Changed LoadStudentEnrollments() to load all students with left join
- Previously only students WITH enrollments were displayed
- Now using Students.Include(Enrollments) instead of Enrollments.Include(Student)

Test Results: 40/40 passing (100%)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: reorganize dev-docs/migration-tests/ into project/runNN/ structure

Reorganized the migration-tests folder from a flat directory with
inconsistent naming into a clean hierarchical structure:

- wingtiptoys/run01-run17/ (16 runs, run07 skipped)
- contosouniversity/run01-run18/ (18 runs, renumbered to fix collisions)

Key changes:
- All runs now in project/runNN/ folders with zero-padded numbers
- Merged standalone .md reports into run folders as summary.md
- Renamed lowercase report.md to REPORT.md for consistency
- Removed 5 duplicate root-level screenshots (identical to run13)
- Resolved Contoso run11/12 numbering collisions (Mar 9 vs Mar 10
  runs with same number); Mar 10+ runs renumbered 13-18
- Completely rewrote README.md with all 18 Contoso runs documented
- Created placeholder for run18 (original files were untracked)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: update Beast history and file reorg decision

Appended migration-tests reorganization learnings to Beast history.
Created decision inbox entry for the folder restructuring.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(ai-team): log session, merge 3 decisions

Session: 2026-03-11-service-extensions-and-exec-summary
Requested by: Jeffrey T. Fritz

Changes:
- Logged session to .ai-team/log/
- Merged 3 decision(s) from inbox into decisions.md
- Propagated updates to agent history files (beast, cyclops, forge, jubilee, rogue, colossus)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Run 18b: Re-run migration after Checkout pattern fix

Re-ran Layer 1 migration after Test-UnconvertiblePage script fix
(path-based Checkout detection). ShoppingCart.razor is STILL stubbed
due to a second false positive: the 'PayPal' pattern matches an image
URL and alt text in markup, not actual PayPal SDK code.

Results:
- Layer 1: 32 files, 303 transforms, 1.58s
- Stubs: 6 (unchanged - PayPal false positive persists)
- Build: 6 errors (same Parameter TODO annotation bug)
- Report updated with Run 18b section
- Decision filed for PayPal pattern fix

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: Run 18c  ShoppingCart GridView fix confirmed after PayPal pattern removal

ShoppingCart.razor now contains full GridView with BoundField/TemplateField
markup instead of a stub. Two false-positive patterns in Test-UnconvertiblePage
have been fixed:
1. 'Checkout' content pattern replaced with path-based '^Checkout[/\\]'
2. 'PayPal' content pattern removed (matched image URLs, not SDK code)

Results:
- UnconvertibleStub count: 5 (Checkout/ only, down from 6)
- Transforms: 314 (up from 303)
- Layer 1 timing: 1.51s
- Build: 6 pre-existing errors in ProductDetails/ProductList (P1, separate)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: Run 18 GridView ShoppingCart breakthrough with screenshots and exec summary update

- ShoppingCart.razor now uses BWFC GridView with BoundField/TemplateField
- Fixed GetCartId() to read WingtipToysCartId cookie (was hardcoded 'sample-cart')
- Fixed migration script: removed 'Checkout' and 'PayPal' false-positive patterns
- Regenerated performance charts with Run 18 data (1.51s L1, 314 transforms)
- 4 screenshots captured: Home, Products, Shopping Cart (GridView), Product Details
- Executive Summary updated: Run 18 screenshots, GridView milestone, improved strategy section
- Run 18 REPORT.md updated with build success and Layer 2 fix documentation
- ServiceCollectionExtensions: merged with WebApplicationExtensions
- Added AspxRewriteMiddleware, BlazorWebFormsComponentsOptions
- ContosoUniversity folder reorganization

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(ai-team): log Run 18 analysis, merge Forge improvement recommendations

Session: 2026-03-11-run18-improvements
Requested by: Jeffrey T. Fritz

Changes:
- Logged session to .ai-team/log/2026-03-11-run18-improvements.md
- Merged Forge's Run 18 improvement recommendations into decisions.md
- Merged Cyclops Run 18 checkout/PayPal/GridView fix decisions
- Merged Beast chart generation decision
- Merged user directives (eliminate Test-UnconvertiblePage, standardize ItemType, P0-2 approved)
- Consolidated 3 overlapping Test-UnconvertiblePage decisions into 1
- Propagated team updates to Cyclops, Beast, and Forge histories

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: standardize generic type params to ItemType across all BWFC components

Renamed TItemType -> ItemType in DataBoundComponent and SelectHandler.
Renamed TItem -> ItemType in BaseListControl, BulletedList, CheckBoxList,
DropDownList, ListBox, and RadioButtonList (both .razor and .razor.cs files).

This aligns all generic type parameters with the ASP.NET Web Forms
DataBoundControl.ItemType convention, ensuring BWFC's drop-in replacement
promise matches the original attribute names.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

First migration run with:
- Test-UnconvertiblePage eliminated (0 stubs, all pages converted)
- RouteData [Parameter] annotation bug fixed
- ItemType standardized across BWFC library

Results: 1.76s, 348 transforms, 32 files  35 razor files
Build: 184 errors (expected L2 residuals), 122 warnings

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

This reverts commit 58fdc8c43a501704b1d6851379de110557e8409d.

  invocation command, Layer 2 Copilot transform checklist, and pipeline rules
- Fix all TItem references  ItemType throughout bwfc-migration SKILL.md (8 instances)
  to match standardized Web Forms DataBoundControl.ItemType naming
- Update migration-standards SKILL.md: rename 'Layer 2 (Manual)' to

  expand Layer 2 transform list
- Add decision file and update Beast history

* feat: L2 structural transform for ContosoUniversity sample

Convert AfterContosoUniversity from L1-scaffolded Web Forms stubs to
working Blazor components with EF Core SQLite, BWFC component patterns,
and DI registration.

Changes:
- Models: EF Core with data annotations, ContosoUniversityContext (SQLite)
- BLL: 4 logic classes + StudentViewModel using IDbContextFactory
- Pages: About, Students, Courses, Instructors converted to Blazor partials
- Infrastructure: Program.cs with DI, csproj with Sqlite, _Imports.razor
- Cleanup: deleted EDMX artifacts, empty code-behinds
- Build: 0 errors

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(tests+samples): Rename TItem to ItemType in all test and sample files

The ItemType standardization (renamed TItem/TItemType to ItemType across
all generic data-bound components) was not applied to test files and
sample pages. This caused RZ10001 and CS0411 build errors because the
Razor compiler could not resolve the generic type parameter.

Updated 43 files across:
- RadioButtonList, BulletedList, CheckBoxList, DropDownList, ListBox tests
- ToolTipTests (BaseWebFormsComponent)
- All ControlSamples sample pages
- AfterWingtipToys account pages

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: update Service Tree ID guidance for required-field tenants

Step 3a in copilot-studio-setup.md previously told users to leave
Service Tree ID blank. Some enterprise/Microsoft-internal tenants
enforce it as required. Updated guidance to cover both cases with
step-by-step instructions for obtaining an ID from the Service Tree
portal (https://aka.ms/servicetree) and fallback to IT admin.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: joelmartinez <90380+joelmartinez@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix wavefront marking exit boundary tiles as distance 0 (#166)

When the objective is to exit the room (e.g., move north), the wavefront
was marking ALL boundary tiles as distance 0. This allowed the model to
idle at the boundary moving left/right with 0 PBRS rewards.

Fix: wavefront exit targets are now a single center tile one step
off-screen (e.g., center of north exit at y=-1). This creates a gradient
along the exit boundary — lateral movement away from center produces
negative PBRS, pushing the model to converge on the exit center and leave.

Also fixes dungeon exit reverse mappings in _get_exit_tiles to use the
actual boundary tile coordinates (not the door tile coordinates).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix doNotDeploy bypassed when broader target precedes it in the target list (#4688)

`MatchTargetCustomizations` returns on the first matching target. If a
broader-matching customization (e.g. `clusterGroup: all`) is listed
before a `doNotDeploy: true` customization (e.g. `clusterGroup: one`),
the doNotDeploy entry is never evaluated for clusters that match the
broader entry first.

Additionally, `target.DoNotDeploy` (from the main `Match()` call) was
not being checked when no targetCustomization was found.

Add `HasDoNotDeployTarget()` to `BundleMatch`, which scans all
targets—not just the first match—for a `doNotDeploy` flag. Update
`Targets()` in `builder.go` to consult this method before building a
`Target`, and separately honour `target.DoNotDeploy` from the GitRepo
match. Add an integration test covering the first-match bypass scenario.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: jesse.mirza@nslogin.nl <jesse.mirza@ns.nl>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Rework Objectives (#165)

* Add game.yaml

* Convert triforce.json to triforce.yaml

Replace JSON config with YAML for easier editing. Update all loaders
in scenario_wrapper.py and models.py to use yaml.safe_load(). Update
references in comments/help text across train.py, diagnose.py,
scenario_selector.py, and tests.

Also adds missing key treasures to game.yaml (rooms 0x53, 0x72) and
wood-sword cave room for 0x77.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add key-aware Dijkstra routing to GameMap

- find_route() uses expanded state (room, keys, collected_mask) for
  key-aware pathfinding through locked doors
- find_next_rooms() returns all equal-cost first moves for multiple
  objective arrows
- find_rooms_with_treasure() for treasure lookup
- Fix backtracking bug: compare full state tuple, not just room
- Add TestTreasureLookup (5 tests) and TestKeyAwareRouting (7 tests)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add ReachLocation and TreasureObjective classes

New objective selectors that use GameMap for routing:
- _GameMapObjective base class with shared room/cave/fight logic
- ReachLocation: routes to a specific (level, location, in_cave)
- TreasureObjective: routes to collect a named treasure
- objective_params plumbing through scenario/state_change wrappers
- RoomMemory now accepts optional GameMap for item lookup
- Fix cave room in game.yaml (add south exit back to overworld)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add end conditions and update scenarios for new objectives

- ReachedLocation: terminates when agent reaches target location
- CollectedTreasure: terminates when target treasure is collected
  (handles triforce, sword, and generic treasure detection)
- End conditions receive objective_params for parameterized scenarios
- Update overworld-sword to use TreasureObjective(treasure=wood-sword)
- Update dungeon1 to use TreasureObjective(treasure=triforce)
- Lint clean (10/10)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Convert remaining scenarios to new objective types

- skip-sword-to-triforce → TreasureObjective(triforce)
- game-start → TreasureObjective(wood-sword)
- overworld-skip-sword → ReachLocation(dungeon 1 entrance)
- dungeon1-side-room-opening → TreasureObjective(triforce)
- dungeon1-wallmaster → TreasureObjective(triforce)
- dungeon1-skip-opening → TreasureObjective(triforce)
- Only full-game, dungeon1-dungeon2, no-end-conditions remain as
  GameCompletion (multi-objective progression logic deferred)
- Add CollectedTreasure/ReachedLocation end conditions to scenarios
- Update test expectations for new end condition lists

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Always fight/collect in dungeon key rooms, track collected keys

Dungeon rooms with treasure always trigger fight/collect objectives,
not just the target room. Key rooms along the path now prompt the
agent to kill enemies and grab the key before proceeding.

Track collected key rooms so Dijkstra routing skips emptied rooms.
Add collected_keys param to GameMap find_route and find_next_rooms.
Fix heap init bug where initial_mask was not propagated.
Add 2 tests for collected_keys routing behavior.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove some end conditions

* Fix wavefront to flow through locked doors when agent has keys

- Fix DOOR_TILES to include all 4 directions (was missing S: 0x9C-0x9F, W: 0xA0-0xA3)
- Add locked_doors parameter to calculate_wavefront_for_link that temporarily
  adds corridor tiles and exit tiles for locked door directions
- Locked door corridors extend 1 tile deeper than open door corridors to cover
  the full 2-tile depth of locked door tiles
- In objectives, add Direction targets when route goes through a locked door
  that isn't in the room's exits
- In state_change_wrapper, detect locked doors and pass to wavefront when agent
  has keys
- Add tests verifying wavefront is blocked by locked doors without keys and
  flows through them with keys

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Track opened doors to prevent routing flip-flop

When the agent spends a key to open a locked door, the key count decreases
and the Dijkstra re-routes using the static map which still marks the door
as locked. With 0 keys, the route flips to 'go collect a key' even though
the door is already open.

Fix: _GameMapObjective now detects doors opened at runtime by comparing
the static map (locked) against the actual tile state (is_door_locked).
Opened doors are passed to the Dijkstra which treats them as free passages
(cost 1, no key consumed). This prevents objectives from flip-flopping
when keys are spent.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Corrected locked door

* Fix collected key rooms and opened doors in routing

- Clear room_memory.item when room is in _collected_key_rooms to skip
  unnecessary fight/collect logic in already-cleared key rooms
- Pass opened_doors to find_route in TreasureObjective._get_target so
  routing doesn't re-block already-opened doors

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix skip-sword-to-triforce scenario to target triforce

Scenario skips the sword cave, so the objective should be triforce
not wood-sword. Removed end conditions that don't apply (sword-specific
and dungeon entry conditions).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add pyyaml to requirements.txt

The yaml module (used by game_map.py for triforce.yaml and game.yaml)
comes from the pyyaml package which was missing from requirements.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refactor Dijkstra into smaller methods to fix R0915

Extract _build_key_bitmask, _dijkstra, _evaluate_exit, and
_reconstruct_paths from _find_all_routes to reduce statement count.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Convert GameCompletion to use GameMap-based routing

GameCompletion now extends _GameMapObjective instead of ObjectiveSelector.
Replaces hand-coded room hints and old A* with GameMap Dijkstra routing.
Implements sword priority logic (wood -> white -> magic based on health)
and dynamic triforce targeting (closest uncleared dungeon).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix cave transition routing in _get_map_objective

When routing to a target in a cave at the same map location (e.g.,
sword cave at 0x77), get_direction_to returns NONE since the grid
coordinates are identical. Now detects cave transitions (same location,
different in_cave flag) and uses cave_tile as the wavefront target.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use GameCompletion objective for full-game scenario

GameCompletion has sword priority logic — it routes to the sword cave
first, then to triforce pieces. TreasureObjective with treasure:triforce
skips the sword entirely.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove old hand-coded objective routing system

- Delete overworld_to_item, dungeon_to_item, item_to_overworld,
  item_to_dungeon, dungeon_entrances dictionaries
- Delete ObjectiveSelector._get_route_with_astar and
  _enumerate_attached_rooms (replaced by GameMap Dijkstra)
- Move GameMap singleton to ObjectiveSelector so all subclasses
  (including RoomWalk) share it
- Update RoomMemory to always use GameMap for item lookup and
  dungeon entrance detection
- Delete obsolete exploration_objective_analysis.py script
- Remove unused imports (heapq, BoomerangKind, ZeldaItemKind)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(theme): export fixMediaScriptContent for non-React SSR consumers

Exports `fixMediaScriptContent` from `@ngrok/mantle/theme` so legacy Go
services and other non-React servers can inline the media-attribute fix
script directly into SSR HTML without going through the React component.

Updates the Non-React Head docs section with the actual inlined script
content (including comments explaining each of the 4 steps), a note
pointing to the generator functions, and an API reference entry for
`fixMediaScriptContent`. Adds a patch changeset.

* Update packages/mantle/src/components/theme/mantle-style-sheets.tsx

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* address pr comments

---------

fix(ci): capture exit code correctly in monitoring workflows

GitHub Actions uses 'set -e' by default, so when the monitor scripts
exit with code 1 (changes detected), bash aborts before EXIT_CODE=\True
can execute. This means the exit_code output is never set, and the
PR creation condition always evaluates to false.

Fix: wrap the python invocation with set +e / set -e so exit code 1
is captured properly. Applied to both learn-monitor and
regulatory-monitoring workflows.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(ci): add missing pyyaml dependency to monitoring workflows

Both learn-monitor and regulatory-monitoring workflows import
monitoring_shared.py which requires pyyaml for config loading.
The dependency was missing from pip install, causing the scripts
to crash with 'No module named yaml' on every run.
